---
# Render this project via `targets::tar_make()` (the `render_site` target in
# _targets.R), not `quarto render index.qmd` directly — this file reads
# pipeline outputs with tar_read()/tar_load(), so a direct render can use
# stale or missing data if the pipeline hasn't been run.
title: "Manuscript Title"
author:
  - name: "Your Name"
    affiliation: "Your Institution"
date: today
abstract: |
  Write a short abstract of the manuscript here.
---

# Introduction {#sec-intro}

Write your introduction here, citing sources as needed [@example2024].

```{r}
#| label: setup
#| include: false
library(targets)
library(here)
here::i_am("index.qmd")
```

# Methods

We can add full-line equations with `\$\$` and reference them [@eq-poisson]. This uses... LatEX? Mathjax? Note that inline code fences don't get any special styling in Word. 

$$
p(x) = \frac{e^{-\lambda} \lambda^{x}}{x !}
$$ {#eq-poisson}

# Results

You can refer back to sections [@sec-intro] We had to put `{#sec-intro}` after "Introduction" in the header above.

This is a figure @fig-example.

```{r}
#| label: fig-example
#| fig-cap: "Example figure generated from the pipeline. In general, figures should always have captions"
data <- tar_read(simulate_data)
model <- tar_read(calculate_model)
plot(data$x, data$y)
abline(model)
```

This is a table @tbl-example. For some reason, they use different caption variables.

```{r}
#| label: tbl-example
#| tbl-cap: "Example figure generated from the pipeline. In general, figures should always have captions"
data <- tar_read(simulate_data)
model <- tar_read(calculate_model)
plot(data$x, data$y)
abline(model)
```


::: {.content-hidden when-format="html"}
# Supplementary Information

Because notebooks, etc. are provided via the sidebar, they don't show up in HTML or Word versions. So we hide them when the format is HTML.

```{r}
#| label: si-links
#| output: asis

# Auto-generated from whatever's actually in supplementary/ - add a new
# notebook there and it shows up here with no manual list to maintain.
# Excludes notebook-template.qmd (a starting point for new notebooks, not
# content). Docx/typst output only: this whole block is hidden for html
# (the sidebar nav covers it there), and `ext` below only knows how to
# build links for the two non-html formats.
si_files <- list.files(here("supplementary"), pattern = "\\.qmd$")
si_files <- sort(setdiff(si_files, "notebook-template.qmd"))

# TODO: Doesn't work when we run in an int. session as knitr option returns null. worth fixing?
ext <- switch(
  knitr::opts_knit$get("rmarkdown.pandoc.to"),
  docx = "docx",
  typst = "pdf",
  NA_character_
)

if (!is.na(ext)) {
  for (f in si_files) {
    title <- rmarkdown::yaml_front_matter(here("supplementary", f))$title
    base <- tools::file_path_sans_ext(f)
    cat(sprintf("- [%s](supplementary/%s.%s)\n", title, base, ext))
  }
}

# TODO: This works, but the formatting's messy and at least in Word they don't look like links
```

:::


# References

::: {#refs}
:::
